title: story-15-review-churn
description:
This is a continuation of the broader non-converging review-churn pattern, now centered on story 15. In the current window, story 15 became active again with a successful dev run ending at 2026-05-29T00:14:25.357152+00:00 followed immediately by a successful reviewer run ending at 2026-05-29T00:14:39.071898+00:00. The flagged watcher note reports cumulative review_churn at 12 reviewer cycles and 15 dev cycles for story 15, which is well beyond the expected convergence range.

The downstream impact is ongoing cost burn without advancement to completion. In this window alone, the active dev and reviewer runs for story 15 consumed about $0.2523, with the dev run lasting 194.876 seconds and carrying a very large 407559-token input, indicating another full iteration of the loop rather than a brief check. There is no corroborating evidence of failed runs, retry storms, queue skew, tick anomalies, or prompt-placeholder leakage in this window; the issue is structural non-convergence rather than execution failure.

The evidence points to repeated successful dev↔reviewer cycling on one story, not an isolated transient event. Because review_churn is explicitly cumulative and the story is active_in_window, the growing cycle count itself is changed evidence and ongoing harm. The most plausible area is prompt/interaction structure for the dev-review loop, although this concern is descriptive rather than diagnostic.

evidence.summary_excerpt: A successful dev run and a follow-on successful reviewer run completed for story 15 in this window, so that story is actively cycling again. Review churn is now active on story 15 at 12 reviewer cycles, well beyond the ~3-cycle convergence ceiling.

evidence.excerpt: {"noteworthy":"Story 15 is reviewer-active in this window with 12 reviewer cycles and 15 dev cycles, indicating ongoing churn well past the convergence ceiling; other high-cycle stories are parked rather than active."}
Successful dev run for story 15: duration_s=194.876, cost_usd=0.211866, attempt_n=15, tokens_in=407559, tokens_out=5391, model=azure/deepseek-v4-pro.
Successful reviewer run for story 15 immediately followed the dev run: duration_s=13.397, cost_usd=0.04046, attempt_n=12, tokens_in=10934, tokens_out=875.

escalation_reason: Story 15 is in an active non-converging review loop with 12 reviewer cycles and 15 dev cycles, and another dev→reviewer pair occurred in this window ending at 2026-05-29T00:14:39.071898+00:00. L3 judgment is needed because review churn is continuing with measurable additional cost burn (about $0.2523 in this window alone) and the structural pattern is already clear.
